findings.evidence: PR grep: `BernsteinMeasures.lean:914: private lemma tail_setIntegral_tendsto_zero ... Tendsto (fun T => ∫ t in Ioi T, g t) atTop (nhds 0)`; Mathlib grep: `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Integral/IntegralEqImproper.lean:671: theorem tendsto_integral_Ioi_zero (hb : Tendsto b l atTop) : Tendsto (fun i => ∫ x in Ioi (b i), f x ∂μ) l (𝓝 0)`.
findings.fix: Delete `tail_setIntegral_tendsto_zero` and use `MeasureTheory.tendsto_integral_Ioi_zero` with `b := fun T : ℝ => T` and `tendsto_id` at the call site.
findings.issue: `tail_setIntegral_tendsto_zero` reproves the existing Mathlib theorem for tails over `Ioi`; this is direct duplication.

summary: The PR mostly reuses existing Mathlib/TauCeti infrastructure, but it introduces a direct private duplicate of an existing Mathlib tail-integral theorem.
